questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A fluid is flowing through a horizontal pipe of varying cross-section, with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">v</mi><mtext> </mtext><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> at a point where the pressure is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> Pascal. At another point where pressure <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> Pascal its speed is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi>ms</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the density of the fluid is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">ρ</mi><mtext> </mtext><mi>kg</mi><mo>−</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> and the flow is streamline, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
P+12ρv2=P2+12ρV2

P2+12ρv2=12ρV2 ;

V=v2+Pρ
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Two narrow bores of diameter 5.0 mm and 8.0 mm are joined together to form a U-shaped tube open at both ends. If this U-tube contains water, what is the difference in the level of two limbs of the tube.
[Take surface tension of water T=7.3×10-2 N m-1, angle of contact =0, g=10 m s-2 and density of water=1.0×103 kg m-3
questions.solution:
P0-2TR1+h1ρg=P0-2TR2+h2ρg
h1-h2ρg=2T12.5×10-3-14×10-3⇒h1-h2=2.19 mm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A light cylindrical vessel is kept on a horizontal surface. Area of the base is A. A hole of cross-sectional area a is made just at its bottom side. The minimum coefficient of friction necessary to prevent sliding the vessel due to the impact force of the emerging liquid is
questions.solution:
For no sliding  

f≥ρav2
μmg≥ρav2
μρAhg≥ρa2gh
μ≥2aA
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Two small drops of mercury each of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> coalesce to form a single large drop. The ratio of total surface energy before and after the change is
questions.solution:
43πR3+43πR3=43πR'3

R'=213R  …i

Ai=24πR2

Af=4πR'2

UiUf=AiAf=2R2223R2=213
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A small spherical droplet of density <math><mi>d</mi></math> is floating exactly half immersed in a liquid of density <math><mi>ρ</mi></math> and surface tension <math><mi>T</mi><mo>.</mo></math> The radius of the droplet is (take note that the surface tension applies an upward force on the droplet):
questions.solution:
dVg=pV2g+T2πr
⇒d.43πr3g=p.23πr3g+2πrT
⇒23r2g2d-p=2T
⇒r=3T2d-pg
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A water drop of radius 1 cm is broken into 729 equal droplets. If surface tension of water is 75dyne cm-1, then the gain in surface energy upto first decimal place will be

[Given π=3.14]
questions.solution:
The volume of the bigger drop will be, V=43πR3. If the radius of smaller drops is r, using the conservation of volume,

n43πr3=43πR3⇒729×43πr3=43πR3⇒r=R9

The initial surface energy will be,

Ei=4πR2T 

And final surface energy will be,

Ef=n4πr2T=729×4πR92T=36πR2T

Therefore, the change in the surface energy will be,

⇒ΔE=Ef-Ei=32πR2T=32×3.14×1×10-22×75×10-3⇒ΔE=7.5×10-4 J
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The velocity of a small ball of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and density <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>d</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>, when dropped in a container filled with glycerine, becomes constant after some time. If the density of glycerine is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>d</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, then the viscous force acting on the ball, will be
questions.solution:
At the time of terminal velocity ball is in equilibrium i.e. Fg=Fb+Fv

⇒Fv=Fg-Fb

⇒Fv=mg-d2md1g

⇒mg1-d2d1
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A pressure-pump has a horizontal tube of cross-sectional area <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup></math> for the outflow of water at a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The force exerted on the vertical wall just in front of the tube which stops water horizontally flowing out of the tube, is: [given : density of water <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>1000</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>]
questions.solution:
Volume of water coming out of the pipe per sec =Av=10×10-4×20=0.02 m3 s-1

Mass of water hitting the wall per second m=0.02×103=20 kg s-1

As the water hits the wall normal to the wall and stops, the change of momentum of water hitting the wall is =m0--v=mv

=20×20=400 kg m s-2

Since, the change of momentum obtained is per unit time, it is same as the force.  

Thus, the force acting on the wall by water = force experienced by water jet=400 N.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Consider a cylindrical tank of radius 1 m is filled with water. The top surface of water is at 15 m from the bottom of the cylinder. There is a hole on the wall of cylinder at a height of 5 m from the bottom. A force of 5×105 N is applied an the top surface of water using a piston. The speed of efflux from the hole will be :
(given atmospheric pressure PA=1.01×105 Pa, density of water ρw=1000 kg m-3 and gravitational acceleration g=10 m s-2)
questions.solution:
Assuming there is no atmospheric pressure on piston, P1=FπR2 and P2=P0.

Applying Bernoulli's equation at point 1 and 2,

p1+ρgh1+12ρv12=p2+ρgh2+12ρv22

⇒FπR2+ρgh1-h2=P0+12ρv22

⇒v2=2FπR2ρ-2P0ρ+2gh1-h2

v2=2×5×1053.14×12×1000-2×1.01×1051000+2×10×10

≈17.8 m s-1
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A small ball of mass M and density ρ is dropped in a viscous liquid of density ρ0. After some time, the ball falls with a constant velocity. What is the viscous force on the ball?
questions.solution:
The buoyant force is given by 

B=Vρ0g

where V is the volume of the ball. The weight of the ball

W=Mg=Vρg, is acting in the opposite direction of the buoyant force . Hence, at terminal velocity

Mg-B=Fv

⇒B+Fv=Mg

⇒Fv=Vρg-Vρ0g⇒Fv=ρVg(1-ρ0ρ)⇒Fv=Mg(1-ρ0ρ)
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The figure shows a liquid of given density flowing steadily in horizontal tube of varying cross-section. Cross-sectional areas at A is 1.5 cm2, and B is 25 mm2, if the speed of liquid at B is 60 cm s-1 then (PA–PB) is

(Given PA and PB are liquid pressures at A and B points.

Density ρ=1000 kg m-3

A and B are on the axis of tube)
questions.solution:
Using Bernoulli's theorem,

PA+12ρVA2=PB+12ρVB2   ...(i)

Using equation of continuity,

AAVA=ABVB

So, 1.5×10-4×VA=25×10-6×VB

VA=25×10-61.5×10-4×0.6

=110 m s-1

Substituting in equation (i)

⇒PA-PB=100020.62-0.12=10002×0.7×0.5

=175 Pa
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as

Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R

Assertion A : A spherical body of radius (5 ± 0.1) mm having a particular density is falling through a liquid of constant density. The percentage error in the calculation of its terminal velocity is 4%.

Reason R : The terminal velocity of the spherical body falling through the liquid is inversely proportional to its radius. In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.solution:
The terminal velocity is 

VT=29r2gρ-ρ'η⇒VT∝r2

Differentiating and dividing by VT,

⇒dVTVT=2drr=2×0.15

So, the percentage error is

dVTVT×100=0.25×100=4%

As can be seen the terminal velocity is directly proportional to the square of the radius. Hence A is true but R is false.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Speed of a transverse wave on a straight wire (mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6.0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>,</mo></math> length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> and area of cross-section <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1.0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>  is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>90</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the Young's modulus of wire is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>11</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, the extension of wire over its natural length is:
questions.solution:
v=Tμ
⇒T=μv2
⇒μv2A=Y∆ll
⇒∆l=μv2lAY
After substituting value of μ,v,l,A and Y we get
∆l=0.3mm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The area of cross section of the rope used to lift a load by a crane is 2.5×10-4 m2. The maximum lifting capacity of the crane is 10 metric tons. To increase the lifting capacity of the crane to 25 metric tons, the required area of cross section of the rope should be
(take g=10 ms-2)
questions.solution:
Since breaking stress (Maximum lifting capacity) is the property of material, so it will remain same.

Now, breaking stress= Maximum lifting capacity  Area of cross section of rope 

⇒102.5×10-4=25 A

A=625×10-6

=6.25×10-4 m2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A steel wire of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>Y</mi><mi>S</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>11</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> and a copper wire of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>Y</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">C</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>11</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> , both of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> are connected end to end. When stretched by a load, the net elongation is found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The load applied, in Newton, will be: (Given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>22</mn><mn>7</mn></mfrac></math>)
questions.solution:
When two wires are connected end to end, the tension in them will be same.

Change in length using Hooke's law can be found as,

Y=FlA∆l⇒Δl=FlAY

Total change in length can be written as,

Δl=Δl1+Δl2   

⇒Δl=Fl1 A1Y1+Fl2 A2Y2

⇒F=Δll1 A1Y1+l2 A2Y2=1.4×10-33.2π1.4×10-32×2.0×1011+4.4π1.4×10-32×1.1×1011⇒F=1.54×102=154 N
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A wire of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> is hanging from a fixed support. The length changes to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>L</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> when masses <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> are suspended respectively from its free end. Then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
From Hooke's law, ∆L=FLAY⇒∆L∝F. Therefore,

L2-LL1-L=m2gm1g=21  ⇒L=2L1-L2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Under isothermal condition, the pressure of a gas is given by P=aV–3, where a is a constant and V is the volume of the gas. The bulk modulus at constant temperature is equal to
questions.solution:
The data given is 

P=aV-3

Bulk modulus is given by,

B=-dPdVV=-VdPdV

=-VddVaV3

=-V-3aV4

=3aV3=3P
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A mass of 5 kg is connected to a spring. The potential energy curve of the simple harmonic motion executed by the system is shown in the figure. A simple pendulum of length 4 m has the same period of oscillation as the spring system. What is the value of acceleration due to gravity on the planet where these experiments are performed ?
questions.solution:
From the figure, A=2 m

12mω2A2=10
12mω2(2)2=10
mω2=5
5ω2=5
ω2=1
ω=1
T=2πω=2π
2πℓgp=2π
2π4gp=2π
gp=4m/s2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A particle is making simple harmonic motion along the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math>-axis. If at a distances <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> from the mean position the velocities of the particle are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, respectively. The time period of its oscillation is given as:
questions.solution:
v2=ω2 A2-x2

A2=x12+v12ω2=x22+v22ω2

ω2=v22-v12x12-x22

T=2πx12-x22v22-v12
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: In the given figure, a body of mass M is held between two massless springs, on a smooth inclined plane. The free ends of the springs are attached to firm supports. If each spring has spring constant k, the frequency of oscillation of given body is:
questions.solution:
Keq=K1+K2=K+K=2K

T=2πmKeq=2πm2 K

f=1 T=12π2K m
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The point A moves with a uniform speed along the circumference of a circle of radius 0.36 m and covers 30° in 0.1 s. The perpendicular projection P from A on the diameter MN represents the simple harmonic motion of P. The restoration force per unit mass when P touches M will be :
questions.solution:
The point A covers 30° in 0.1 s.
Then, we have π6 rad⟶0.1 s.
Or 1 rad⟶0.1π6 s
Or 2π in→ 0.1×6π×2π
Thus, time taken is T=1.2 sec.

We know that ω=2πT
Then, ω=2π1.2
Restoration force (F)=mω2A.

Then Restoration force per unit mass Fm=ω2 A
Fm=2π1.22×0.36

≅9.87 N
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The variation of displacement with time of a particle executing free simple harmonic motion is shown in the figure.

The potential energy U(x) versus time t plot of the particle is correctly shown in figure:
questions.solution:
Considering→ Spring - mass system

x=x0sinωt

P.E.=12kx2=12kxo2sin2ωt=csin2ωt

⇒PE=1-cos2ωt2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A particle executes simple harmonic motion between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>–</mo><mi>A</mi><mo> </mo><mtext>and</mtext><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>+</mo><mi>A</mi></math>. If time taken by particle to go from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>A</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>; then time taken by particle in going from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>A</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is:
questions.solution:
The position of a particle performing an SHM is given by:

x=A sin(ωt+ϕ)

Since at t=0 s, the particle is at x=0, therefore the initial phase ϕ=0.

At time t=2 s, the particle is at x=A2.

∴ A2=A sin2ω⇒ω=π12 s-1

Therefore, time period

T=2πω=24 s

The total time taken from x=0 to x=A is T4=6 s.

Since the time taken from x=0 to x=A2 is 2 s, therefore the time taken from x=A2 to x=A is 4 s.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For particle P revolving round the centre O with radius of circular path r and regular velocity ω, as shown in below figure, the projection of OP on the x-axis at time t is
questions.solution:
As can be seen from the diagram, the initial phase of the particle is given by

φ=30°= π6

The equation of motion of the particle at its initial position at time t=0 can be written as

xt=rcosθ   ...(1)

where, θ is the angular displacement of the particle at time t=t.

Since, the position of the particle at a later instant of time always lags behind its initial position at time t=0, the equation of motion of the particle at any instant of time is given by

 xt=rcosθ+φ   ...2

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (2) to obtain the required expression

x(t)=r cosωt+π6
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In a resonance tube experiment when the tube is filled with water up to a height of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi><mtext>,</mtext></math> from bottom, it resonates with a given tuning fork. When the water level is raised the next resonance with the same tuning fork occurs at a height of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi><mtext>.</mtext></math> If the velocity of sound in air is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>330</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mtext>,</mtext></math> the tuning fork frequency is :
questions.solution:
λ2=24.5-17=7.5 cm

f=Vλ

=330×10015=2200 Hz
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A transverse wave is represented by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>k</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>cm</mi></math>. The value of wavelength (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math>) for which the wave velocity becomes equal to the maximum particle velocity, will be
questions.solution:
The given equation is y=2sinωt-kx

The maximum particle velocity =Aω

And the wave velocity =ωk

For wave velocity to be equal to maximum particle velocity

ωk=Aω

⇒k=1 A

Since, k=2πλ

λ=2πA=4π cm.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: The velocity of sound in a gas, in which two wavelengths <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>08</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>16</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> produce <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn></math> beats in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>, will be
questions.solution:
If the velocity of the sound in the medium is v then, f1=v4.08 and f2=v4.16

Now beat frequency, 

fb=Δf=f1-f2=4012=103 Hz⇒v14.08-14.16=103 Hz⇒707.2 m s-1
